rationale: Both candidates independently converge on the same real reviewer concern (dsocek's 'derive the component list from existing per-pipeline metadata instead of hardcoding'), giving them comparable recall, and Y adds a plausible, well-evidenced test-coverage gap (no SDXL-pipeline-level test) that X misses. However, Y's verdict is 'REQUEST CHANGES' — printed three times, redundantly — despite its own findings being labeled minor/nit, directly contradicting the ground truth of two unconditional LGTM approvals and a merged PR; this is a real calibration failure that undercuts precision. X's 'COMMENT, approve-level, non-blocking' verdict matches the actual outcome, and it also correctly credits the PR body's before/after validation images, addressing xuechendi's validation-result ask that Y ignores entirely.
